$schema: Steal Report Output Schema — R58 (inspired by HV-Analysis)
description: Structural completeness gate for steal reports. Every field must be explicitly filled or marked N/A with reason. Empty/skipped fields are treated as incomplete.

report_header.title: string — <Project Name> Steal Report
report_header.source: string — repo URL or article URL
report_header.category: enum: Framework | Self-Evolving | Module | Survey | Skill-System

tldr: string — Problem space + solution pattern in 1-2 sentences. NOT what the project does — why it's worth stealing from.

six_dimensional_scan.description: Each dimension must be explicitly assessed. Empty = 'N/A: <reason>', not skipped.

security_governance.na_reason: string | null — required if status is N/A

path_dependency.description: R58 addition — assess what locked the project into its current path
locking_decisions: string — early choices that locked direction
missed_forks: string — key points where alternative paths existed
path_dependency.self_reinforcement: string — what mechanisms deepen the current path
path_dependency.lesson_for_us: string — learn the chosen path (copy) or learn from the lock-in (avoid)

p0_must_steal.mechanism: string — the actual how, with code snippet reference

categories_hit: enum: pitfall_memory | judgment_heuristics | relationship_graph | hidden_context | failure_memory | unique_behavioral_patterns

adjacent_discoveries: string — libraries, techniques, communities, structural transfer opportunities
meta_insights: string — strategic observations that transcend individual patterns
